purpose: AI-friendly index for selecting and copying BAS/Mako/Xedge examples.

examples.title: AJAX
examples.summary: Minimal LSP AJAX example where browser keypresses are posted to Lua and returned as JSON.
goodFor: submitting browser events or settings to Lua/LSP

appRoots.label: AJAX demo
appRoots.summary: Runnable Mako app root for the AJAX request-response example.

examples.title: REST / AJAX / RPC Over WebSockets
examples.summary: Browser-to-Lua request/response calls over a persistent WebSocket transport.

appRoots.label: AJAX over WebSockets
appRoots.summary: WebSocket service and browser clients for asynchronous RPC-style calls.

examples.title: Lua Authentication Examples
examples.summary: Three BAS authentication patterns for protecting an app root, a subdirectory, or individual pages.

notes: Digest/basic credentials may be cached by browsers while testing.

appRoots.label: Root-protected auth
appRoots.summary: Authenticator applied to the full application root.

appRoots.label: Semiautomatic page auth
appRoots.summary: Pages that call authentication manually through shared page logic.

appRoots.label: Subdirectory auth
appRoots.summary: Authenticator applied to only the protected subdirectory branch.

examples.title: AWS IoT Core MQTT
examples.summary: MQTT client example for connecting to AWS IoT Core with ALPN and mutual TLS.
goodFor: AWS IoT Core MQTT connection examples
publishing JSON data to cloud topics

notes: Requires AWS endpoint, CA certificate, device certificate, and private key before it will connect.

appRoots.label: AWS MQTT client
appRoots.summary: Startup-only MQTT client application for AWS IoT Core.

examples.title: CGI Plugin and Examples
examples.summary: Lua CGI compatibility layer using forkpty to run external CGI scripts.

notes: Depends on the forkpty plugin and is intended for Linux, macOS, and QNX.

appRoots.label: CGI bridge
appRoots.summary: App root that mounts a CGI directory into the BAS virtual file system.

examples.title: Command Environment
examples.summary: Examples showing request/response environment behavior with includes and forwards.

appRoots.label: Forward command environment
appRoots.summary: LSP forward-chain example.

appRoots.label: Include command environment
appRoots.summary: LSP include example with shared header and footer pages.

examples.title: CryptoIO
examples.summary: AES-GCM encrypted IO wrapper with an encrypted WebDAV/file-server example.
goodFor: WebDAV file server with encrypted backing storage

appRoots.label: Encrypted file service
appRoots.summary: App root containing the CryptoIO module and encrypted file server setup.

examples.title: CSRF Prevention
examples.summary: Small LSP form example that checks trusted host/referrer state before accepting a request.

appRoots.label: CSRF form demo
appRoots.summary: Self-contained app root with preload handshake and protected form page.

examples.title: Docex Shopping Cart
examples.summary: Small shopping-cart example using LSP pages and a startup preload script.

appRoots.label: Shopping cart
appRoots.summary: Runnable cart app root.

examples.title: Dynamic Navigation Menu
examples.summary: Authenticated multi-page dashboard shell with JSON-backed users and dynamic navigation.
goodFor: form login with hashed JSON user data

appRoots.label: Authenticated navigation dashboard
appRoots.summary: Dashboard-style app root with login handling, menu pages, and source viewer.

examples.title: Email and Logging
examples.summary: Examples for sending email, parsing EML, and mailing logged server errors.

notes: Several variants use Mako log SMTP settings from mako.conf.

appRoots.label: EML parser
appRoots.summary: EML parser example and supporting Lua modules.

appRoots.label: HTML email
appRoots.summary: Startup script that sends an HTML email using configured SMTP settings.

appRoots.label: Emailed log errors
appRoots.summary: Logging example with an LSP page that can trigger emailed error reporting.

appRoots.label: Plain text email
appRoots.summary: Startup script that sends a plain text email using configured SMTP settings.

examples.title: ESP32 Xedge32 Examples
examples.summary: Collection of Xedge32 examples for GPIO, sensors, servo control, cameras, MQTT, and audio.
goodFor: camera, sensor, LED, and servo examples

notes: .xlua files are important for Xedge32 startup behavior; Mako can store them but does not auto-execute them.
Copying the whole directory can start multiple XLua apps on Xedge32, so select files deliberately.

appRoots.label: ESP32 examples bundle
appRoots.summary: Bundle of Xedge32-oriented LSP and XLua examples.

appRoots.label: ESP32 sensor simulator
appRoots.summary: Simulator support for ESP32 examples.

examples.title: WebDAV and Web File Server
examples.summary: Ready-to-run WebDAV/Web File Manager example with basic authentication.

appRoots.label: Web file server
appRoots.summary: App root that mounts WebDAV/Web File Manager at /fs/.

examples.title: File Server Single Sign-On
examples.summary: OpenID Connect single sign-on flow for a Web File Server style app.

notes: Requires external identity-provider configuration and valid client credentials.

appRoots.label: SSO file server
appRoots.summary: SSO app root with Microsoft Entra style login flow and file-server access.

examples.title: GitHub IO
examples.summary: GitHub repository mounted as a BAS file system through a Lua IO implementation.

notes: The shipped preload intentionally errors until credentials and repository settings are configured.

appRoots.label: GitHub IO file system
appRoots.summary: App root containing the GitHub IO module and example mount.

examples.title: Basic HTML Form
examples.summary: Beginner LSP form example where one page renders and processes submitted fields.
goodFor: form submission from browser to server

appRoots.label: HTML form
appRoots.summary: Single LSP page for rendering and processing a basic HTML form.

examples.title: htmx Introduction
examples.summary: Server-rendered htmx example where LSP returns HTML fragments for browser updates.
goodFor: updating page fragments without custom JavaScript

appRoots.label: htmx introduction
appRoots.summary: Browser page and LSP fragment endpoint for the htmx introduction.

examples.title: JSON File Server Authentication
examples.summary: Web file server using JSON-backed users, digest authentication, request login, and ACL examples.
goodFor: Web File Manager with user roles

appRoots.label: JSON-authenticated file server
appRoots.summary: App root with JSON user database setup and Web File Manager mount.

examples.title: Light Dashboard
examples.summary: Embedded dashboard template variants with SMQ-based light control and optional authentication.

notes: Custom variant is the best starting point for adapting the dashboard template.

appRoots.label: Custom dashboard
appRoots.summary: Customizable dashboard variant with SMQ slider and CMS-like Lua templates.

appRoots.label: HTMX dashboard
appRoots.summary: HTMX-styled dashboard variant with SMQ light-control endpoint.

appRoots.label: Pure CSS dashboard
appRoots.summary: Original dashboard variant with static assets and SMQ light-control endpoint.

examples.title: Lua Debug
examples.summary: VS Code debugging workflow for Lua, LSP, preload scripts, and Mako-loaded apps.
goodFor: VS Code debug setup for Mako

notes: Debug workflow is centered on local Mako Server and VS Code.

appRoots.label: Debug file server
appRoots.summary: File-server app used by the debugging walkthrough.

appRoots.label: Lua debug app
appRoots.summary: Debuggable LSP app with preload script and Lua module.

examples.title: MinnowSim
examples.summary: Embedded single-page application simulator with LSP entry point and preload logic.
goodFor: small BAS app roots without a www wrapper

notes: No local MinnowSim README was present; metadata is inferred from the root README and app markers.

appRoots.label: MinnowSim app
appRoots.summary: Self-contained app root with index.lsp and preload script.

examples.title: MQTT Broker
examples.summary: Lua MQTT broker example with TLS/plain listeners and broker callback hooks.

notes: Default listeners may conflict with existing services on ports 1883, 1884, or 8883.

appRoots.label: MQTT broker app
appRoots.summary: App root containing broker module and startup configuration.

examples.title: MySQL and Redis Drivers
examples.summary: BAS-compatible MySQL and Redis drivers adapted from OpenResty with test LSP pages.
notFor: embedded RTOS targets without database services

notes: The reusable driver bundle is packaged from MysqlAndRedis/MySQL and loaded alongside MysqlAndRedis/www.

appRoots.label: MySQL and Redis example package
appRoots.summary: Package root containing both the driver bundle and the LSP test pages.

examples.title: OAuth 2.0 Example
examples.summary: GitHub OAuth 2.0 client flow converted from PHP to LSP.

notes: Requires OAuth application configuration, callback URL, and credentials.

appRoots.label: GitHub OAuth client
appRoots.summary: Single LSP GitHub OAuth client example.

examples.title: PostgreSQL
examples.summary: Form-driven PostgreSQL example using a shared helper object and deferred response flow.
goodFor: background SQL work from LSP pages

notes: Requires PostgreSQL service and pgsql.so availability.

appRoots.label: PostgreSQL form app
appRoots.summary: App root containing PostgreSQL startup helper and form page.

examples.title: QNX PPS SMQ Bridge
examples.summary: Reusable Lua module that bridges QNX Persistent Publish Subscribe topics to SMQ.
goodFor: browser access to QNX publish/subscribe data

notes: This directory provides a reusable QNX module rather than a standalone runnable app.

examples.title: RADIUS Authentication
examples.summary: BAS authenticator example backed by a RADIUS client.
goodFor: protecting LSP pages with external authentication

notes: Requires access to a RADIUS server and matching shared secret.

appRoots.label: RADIUS-protected app
appRoots.summary: Protected landing page and RADIUS-backed authenticator setup.

examples.title: Require Search Path Test
examples.summary: Small example showing how a BAS app can add module search paths and require Lua modules.

appRoots.label: Require test
appRoots.summary: App root demonstrating Lua module loading from app directories.

examples.title: RESTful Services
examples.summary: Lua REST router example mounted under /api with JSON-style user endpoints.
goodFor: server-side API examples without a large UI

appRoots.label: REST API app
appRoots.summary: App root with REST router module and API startup script.

examples.title: SMQ Blob Arena
examples.summary: Small AI-generated multiplayer browser game variants using SMQ for realtime state.

appRoots.label: Codex Blob Arena
appRoots.summary: Codex-generated Blob Arena SMQ game variant.

appRoots.label: Gemini Blob Arena
appRoots.summary: Gemini-generated Blob Arena SMQ game variant.

examples.title: SMQ Cluster
examples.summary: Local SMQ clustering and redundancy experiment with chat and autoconfiguration endpoints.

notes: Cluster behavior is intended as an experiment and may require multiple local instances.

appRoots.label: SMQ cluster
appRoots.summary: SMQ cluster app root with chat and autoconfiguration pages.

examples.title: SMQ IoT Examples
examples.summary: SMQ bundle containing chat and browser-based LED-control examples.

appRoots.label: SMQ IoT bundle
appRoots.summary: SMQ app root with chat, basic chat, and LED-control pages.

examples.title: SMQ Light Switch and Light Bulb
examples.summary: Companion browser apps showing SMQ light switch and light bulb messaging.
goodFor: device setting control with a browser UI
light switch and light bulb messaging

appRoots.label: SMQ light switch and bulb
appRoots.summary: Browser switch and bulb pages connected through an SMQ endpoint.

examples.title: SMQ One-to-One
examples.summary: Direct browser-to-server and client-specific SMQ messaging example.

appRoots.label: SMQ one-to-one
appRoots.summary: Small SMQ app with preload broker support and one browser page.

examples.title: REST / AJAX / RPC Over SMQ
examples.summary: Browser-to-server RPC over SMQ with a small JavaScript RPC client and Lua server module.

appRoots.label: SMQ RPC
appRoots.summary: SMQ broker, Lua RPC module, and browser RPC client.

examples.title: Sockets and WebSockets
examples.summary: Bundle of TCP, UDP, NTP, WebSocket, proxy, and Eliza socket examples.

notes: Some examples are socket- or platform-dependent.

appRoots.label: Socket examples bundle
appRoots.summary: App root containing the socket examples landing page and endpoint pages.

examples.title: Sparkplug Client
examples.summary: Sparkplug 3.0 MQTT client documentation with Edge of Network and explorer startup scripts.
goodFor: Sparkplug Edge of Network node examples

notes: EoN run command is inferred from the app marker; README explicitly documents SparkplugExplorer.

appRoots.label: Sparkplug EoN node
appRoots.summary: Edge of Network node startup script using the Sparkplug client library.

appRoots.label: Sparkplug Explorer
appRoots.summary: Console-oriented Sparkplug traffic explorer startup script.

examples.title: SQLite Shared Connection
examples.summary: SQLite example focused on shared access paths, repeated writes, and locking behavior.

appRoots.label: SQLite shared connection
appRoots.summary: App root demonstrating shared SQLite access from LSP.

examples.title: Lua SQLite Database Tutorial
examples.summary: Beginner form-driven SQLite workflow using one LSP page and a preload initializer.

appRoots.label: SQLite tutorial
appRoots.summary: Small SQLite form app for inserts and rendering results.

examples.title: Basic Wiki Engine
examples.summary: Small wiki that maps URLs to SQLite-backed content through a BAS directory function.

appRoots.label: SQLite wiki
appRoots.summary: Wiki app root with SQLite initialization and LSP edit flows.

examples.title: TraceLogger Client
examples.summary: Local client that connects to and forwards trace output from another BAS-enabled device.
goodFor: watching BAS device logs from Mako

notes: Requires editing TraceLogger/mako.conf to point at the peer device.

appRoots.label: TraceLogger client
appRoots.summary: App root that starts the TraceLogger client connection.

examples.title: Asynchronous Upload
examples.summary: Concurrent upload example using ba.create.upload() with drag-and-drop and form upload UI.

notes: More complex than the blocking upload variant and intended for concurrent upload handling.

appRoots.label: Asynchronous upload
appRoots.summary: App root with upload directory object and browser upload UI.

examples.title: Blocking Upload
examples.summary: Simpler upload example using blocking request APIs for multipart and raw drag-and-drop uploads.

notes: Uses blocking request APIs and should run on a server with threaded request handling.

appRoots.label: Blocking upload
appRoots.summary: App root with blocking upload handlers and browser upload UI.

examples.title: Linux Web Shell
examples.summary: Browser terminal over SMQ backed by a server-side pseudo-terminal process.

notes: Linux-oriented because it depends on ba.forkpty().

appRoots.label: Web shell
appRoots.summary: App root with xterm.js UI and SMQ-connected shell process.

examples.title: BAS WebAuthn
examples.summary: Passwordless WebAuthn module and two Mako examples for registration and login.

notes: Run from the WebAuthn directory with mako so mako.conf loads WebAuthnModule plus the selected active example.
Example 2 requires email or manual registration-link handling.

appRoots.label: WebAuthn configured package
appRoots.summary: Package root containing mako.conf, the reusable module app, and both WebAuthn examples.
